name: Refactor code across multiple files
task: Refactor /tmp/eval_refactor/storage.py so it stores MULTIPLE users. save_user(name, email) must APPEND a new user record to the persisted collection (not overwrite it); if /tmp/eval_refactor/users.json does not yet exist, start from an empty collection. Rename load_user to load_users (plural) so it returns the full list of all saved users, in insertion order, as a JSON array of objects each shaped exactly {"name": <str>, "email": <str>}. Persist users.json as that JSON array. Then update /tmp/eval_refactor/app.py to use the new API: save two users — first ("Alice", "alice@example.com"), then ("Bob", "bob@example.com") — load them back with load_users, and print each user's name on its own line (Alice then Bob).

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_refactor && rm -f users.json && OUT=$(python3 app.py) && echo "$OUT" | grep -q 'Alice' && echo "$OUT" | grep -q 'Bob' && python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/tmp/eval_refactor/users.json')); assert isinstance(d, list), 'users.json must be a JSON array, got '+type(d).__name__; assert len(d)==2, 'expected exactly 2 users, got '+str(len(d)); assert d[0]['name']=='Alice' and d[0]['email']=='alice@example.com', 'user0 '+repr(d[0]); assert d[1]['name']=='Bob' and d[1]['email']=='bob@example.com', 'user1 '+repr(d[1]); print('OK')"
